explanation: The LTL property states that globally, if the ventilator is in PSV mode and the patient has not triggered a breath within the apnea lag time (indicated by 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime'), then eventually the ventilator will transition to PCV mode and its parameters (respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio) will be set to their apnea backup values ('RR_equalTo_RR_AP', 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'). This precisely captures the condition-consequence relationship described in the natural language input.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is currently in PSV Mode (PSVMode) AND the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowed apnea lag time (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), then eventually (F), the ventilator shall switch to PCV Mode (PCVMode) AND the respiratory rate (RR) will be equal to the apnea respiratory rate (RR_AP), AND the inspiratory pressure (P_insp) will be equal to the inspiratory pressure for apnea (P_inspAP), AND the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E) will be equal to the apnea I:E ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This accurately captures the conditional transition from PSV Mode to PCV Mode with specific parameters if an apnea event is detected.

The property states that globally, if the ventilator is in PSV mode and the breath timer indicates that the apnea lag time has been exceeded (meaning the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowed window), then eventually the ventilator must switch to PCV mode, and simultaneously, the respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and I:E ratio must be set to their respective apnea backup values (RR_AP, P_inspAP, and I:E_AP). This accurately captures the conditional transition from PSV mode to PCV mode with specific parameters when an apnea event (lack of breath within the apnea lag) is detected.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV mode AND the breath timer indicates that the allowed apnea lag time has been met or exceeded (meaning the patient has not triggered a breath within the apnea trigger window), then eventually the system will switch to PCV mode, and simultaneously, the respiratory rate (RR) will be set to RR_AP, the inspiratory pressure (P_insp) will be set to P_inspAP, and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (I:E) will be set to I:E_AP. This captures the requirement for an apnea backup ventilation in PSV mode.

The LTL property captures the requirement that globally (G), if the system is currently in 'PSVMode' and the 'breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime' condition is met (indicating that the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowed apnea lag time), then eventually (F), the ventilator must switch to 'PCVMode' and simultaneously set the respiratory rate to 'RR_equalTo_RR_AP', the inspiratory pressure to 'P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP', and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio to 'ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP'. This accurately reflects the specified apnea backup ventilation behavior in PSV mode.

The property states that globally, if the system is in PSV Mode and the breath timer indicates that the apnea lag time has been met or exceeded (meaning the patient has not triggered a breath within the apnea trigger window), then it must eventually transition to PCV Mode. Furthermore, when it transitions to PCV Mode, the respiratory rate, inspiratory pressure, and inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio must be set to their respective apnea backup values (RR_AP, P_inspAP, and I:E_AP). This precisely captures the 'PSV Mode: If the patient does not trigger a breath within the time of the apnea trigger window (apnea lag) the ventilator shall switch to PCV mode (apnea backup ventilation) with respiratory rate RR_AP, inspiratory pressure P_inspAP , and the ratio between inspiratory time and Expiratory time I:E_AP' requirement.

The property states that globally (G), if the system is currently operating in PSV mode (PSVMode) AND the breathing time has exceeded or become equal to the apnea lag time (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), indicating the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowed window, THEN eventually (F) the system must transition to PCV mode (PCVMode) AND set the respiratory rate to RR_AP (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), set the inspiratory pressure to P_inspAP (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), AND set the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio to I:E_AP (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This directly translates the 'If ... then ... shall' structure for a global requirement.

The LTL property states that globally (G), if the system is in PSV Mode (PSVMode) and the breath timer indicates that the apnea lag time has been reached or exceeded without a new breath (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), then eventually (F), the system must switch to PCV Mode (PCVMode) and simultaneously set the respiratory rate to RR_AP (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), the inspiratory pressure to P_inspAP (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio to I:E_AP (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This directly captures the requirement that upon detecting apnea in PSV mode, the ventilator must transition to PCV mode with specific backup ventilation parameters.

This property captures the requirement that in PSV mode, if a patient fails to trigger a breath within the specified apnea lag time, the ventilator must eventually switch to PCV mode and apply specific apnea backup ventilation parameters. The "G" operator ensures this rule is always observed. The "PSVMode" proposition acts as a precondition. The "breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime" proposition captures the condition of the patient not triggering a breath within the apnea window. The "F" operator signifies that the consequent actions (switching to PCV mode and setting parameters) will eventually occur. The conjunction of "PCVMode", "RR_equalTo_RR_AP", "P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP", and "ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP" ensures all specified backup parameters are applied when the mode switch occurs.

The property states that globally (G), if the ventilator is in PSV Mode (PSVMode) AND the breath timer indicates that the patient has not triggered a breath within the allowed apnea lag time (breathingTime_greaterThanOrEqual_apneaLagTime), THEN eventually (F) the ventilator will switch to PCV mode (PCVMode) and its parameters will be set to the apnea backup values, specifically the respiratory rate (RR_equalTo_RR_AP), the inspiratory pressure (P_insp_equalTo_P_inspAP), and the inspiratory-to-expiratory ratio (ItoE_equalTo_ItoE_AP). This accurately captures the requirement that upon detecting an apnea event in PSV mode, the system transitions to PCV mode with specific backup settings.
